title: Ranunculus supramedialis
intro: Ranunculus supramontanus, also known as Ranunculus supramedialis, is a plant species that belongs to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae) and is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant is known to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Ranunculus supramedialis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Ranunculus supramedialis include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which are used topically for wound healing and skin care.
taxonomy: Ranunculus supramedialis is classified within the family Ranunculaceae, commonly known as the buttercup family. This family comprises approximately 1,900 species, including Ranunculus supramedialis. As a member of the Ranunculaceae family, Ranunculus supramedialis belongs to the order Ranunculales. The family and order classification reflect the plant's unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other plants. Ranunculus supramedialis is also classified within the genus Ranunculus and the species Ranunculus supramedialis.
